text: Explaining to the landlord: "The basement has accumulated {{c1::mold and mildew}} due to the high {{c2::moisture levels}}, so we need a dehumidifier."
explanation: Mold and mildew refer to fungal growths caused by dampness. **Moisture** refers to wetness or condensation in the air.
usage:
Pattern: accumulate mold due to high moisture / run a dehumidifier
  • High moisture levels in the bathroom will cause mold and mildew.
  • We bought a dehumidifier to keep the basement dry.
spanish: Explicándole al propietario: "El sótano ha acumulado moho y humedad debido a los altos niveles de humedad, por lo que necesitamos un deshumidificador".

text: Troubleshooting the setup: "The smart lock has a {{c1::sync issue}} with the hub, so the door sensor doesn't {{c2::trigger the security routine}}."
explanation: Sync issue refers to synchronization failure. **Trigger a routine** means to automatically start a sequence of pre-programmed actions.
usage:
Pattern: have a sync issue / trigger a routine based on [event]
  • My phone is having a sync issue with the server.
  • Connecting the sensor will trigger the alarm routine.
spanish: Resolviendo problemas de la configuración: "La cerradura inteligente tiene un problema de sincronización con el concentrador, por lo que el sensor de la puerta no activa la rutina de seguridad".

text: Discussing the rent: "We need to pay the first month's utilities and the {{c1::security deposit}} before we can inspect the {{c2::floor plan}}."
explanation: Security deposit is a sum of money paid in advance to protect the landlord against damage. **Floor plan** is the scale diagram showing room layouts.
usage:
Pattern: pay a security deposit / check the floor plan
  • The landlord returned our security deposit when we moved out.
  • Let's study the floor plan to decide where to put the sofa.
spanish: Discutiendo el alquiler: "Debemos pagar los servicios del primer mes y el depósito de seguridad antes de poder inspeccionar el plano de distribución de la casa".

text: Instructing the barista: "If your {{c1::grind size}} is too coarse, it causes {{c2::channeling}}, resulting in a watery crema."
explanation: Grind size determines the speed of water flow. **Channeling** occurs when water finds paths of least resistance through the coffee puck instead of extracting evenly.
usage:
Pattern: adjust the grind size / prevent channeling during extraction
  • If the grind size is too fine, the espresso will extract too slowly.
  • Tamping the coffee evenly prevents channeling.
spanish: Instruyendo al barista: "Si el tamaño de la molienda es demasiado grueso, causa canalización, lo que resulta en una crema aguada".

text: Explaining the game: "This is a {{c1::turn-based game}} with specific card mechanics, including a roll of dice to resolve any {{c2::tie-breaker}}."
explanation: Turn-based denotes a gameplay mechanic where players take turns. **Tie-breaker** is the rule or action that determines a winner when scores are equal.
usage:
Pattern: turn-based game / resolve a tie-breaker
  • Civilization is a famous turn-based strategy game.
  • The team won the match in a sudden-death tie-breaker.
spanish: Explicando el juego: "Este es un juego por turnos con mecánicas de cartas específicas, que incluye un lanzamiento de dados para resolver cualquier desempate".

text: Don't forget to {{c1::take out the trash}} before the garbage truck arrives tomorrow morning.
explanation: To 'take out the trash' means to carry refuse or garbage containers from inside the house to the outdoor collection spot.
usage:
Pattern: take out the trash / take out the rubbish
  • It is your turn to take out the trash tonight.
  • I took out the trash before going to bed.
spanish: No olvides sacar la basura antes de que llegue el camión de la basura mañana por la mañana.

text: I need to {{c1::run some errands}}, including dropping off the dry cleaning and buying groceries.
explanation: To 'run errands' means to take short trips to complete daily tasks or chores outside the house (e.g. post office, grocery store, cleaners).
usage:
Pattern: run errands / do errands
  • I have to run some errands this afternoon, so I'll be out of the office.
  • She spent the whole Saturday running errands.
spanish: Necesito hacer algunos mandados/recados, incluyendo dejar la ropa en la tintorería y comprar víveres.

text: The kitchen sink is leaking, so I need to {{c1::call a plumber}} to fix it.
explanation: To 'call a plumber' is a standard collocation for contacting a professional who installs and repairs pipes, fixtures, and water systems.
usage:
Pattern: call a plumber / hire a plumber
  • We had to call a plumber to unclog the drain.
  • The plumber charged $100 just to inspect the pipes.
spanish: El fregadero de la cocina gotea, así que necesito llamar a un plomero para que lo arregle.

text: We need to {{c1::stock up on}} toilet paper and paper towels this weekend.
explanation: To 'stock up on' means to buy a large quantity of something (usually household supplies or food) to keep for future use.
usage:
Pattern: stock up on [goods]
  • People stocked up on water and canned food before the storm.
  • I always stock up on coffee when it goes on sale.
spanish: Necesitamos abastecernos de papel higiénico y toallas de papel este fin de semana.

text: Could you please {{c1::put away the groceries}} in the pantry?
explanation: To 'put away groceries' means to place purchased food items in their proper storage locations (fridge, freezer, cabinets, pantry) after shopping.
usage:
Pattern: put away the groceries
  • Help me put away the groceries before the ice cream melts.
  • She was busy putting away the groceries.
spanish: ¿Podrías por favor guardar las compras en la despensa?
